### 5. Lindy AI

Lindy AI

Lindy AI is a workflow and automation builder designed for customer support and sales teams. Their "AI employees" can integrate with your existing tech stack to complete tasks on your behalf. [...] ## 8 best AI agent builders and tools in 2026

Here are the best AI agent builder:

Alright, let's go over each of these.

### 1. Gumloop

Gumloop

Gumloop is an AI agent builder and automation platform built for enterprise companies. It's used by some big teams at companies like Shopify, Instacart, and Webflow, across multiple organizations like marketing, sales, opations, finance, customer service, HR, and admin.

What sets Gumloop apart from most of the platforms on this list is how teams actually work in it. It's not just a single player tool where one person owns the build locally or on their own account. Anyone on your team with the right permissions can create an agent or skill, and the rest of the org can run it. [...] Whether you're a customer support team trying to get help with answering more support tickets, a marketing team trying to get help with doing competitor research and generating reports, or a solo creator who needs help responding to business inquiries, there's an AI agent for everything.

I like to think of AI agents as junior level employees that can not only follow your instructions but also begin to make their own decisions without you being involved. This means to get the most out of AI agents, you should deeply understand an existing workflow that you want to automate.

results.title: 11 AI Agent Builders I Tested in 2026: Best No-Code Platform
results.content:
## How to Choose the Right AI Agent Builder for Your Team

With 11 platforms tested, picking the right one depends on your specific situation. Here's the framework I use when recommending AI agent builders to B2B teams.

Start with your use case. If you need customer support deflection, LiveChatAI or Fini AI should be your shortlist. If you need lead-gen automation, Tars or Chatfuel. If you need mobile-first in-app chat, Sendbird. If you need developer-grade control, Botpress.

Evaluate no-code ease. Can your non-technical team members build and maintain the agent? If the answer is no, you'll create a dependency on engineering that slows everything down. LiveChatAI, Chatbase, and Botsonic score highest here. [...] Sales and marketing AI agent: Qualify leads in chat, schedule demos, send tailored follow-ups. Tars and Chatfuel excel here. An AI chatbot for e-commerce can turn visitors into buyers by capturing intent, recommending products, and initiating post-chat follow-ups.

IT and HR AI agent: Reset passwords, provision accounts, answer policy questions, submit PTO. LiveChatAI's internal support chatbot handles these repetitive requests so your IT team focuses on actual infrastructure work.

Ops and finance AI agent: Create purchase orders, flag anomalies, run daily reports, kick off approval flows. According to InData Labs, companies using AI agents for operations have reduced forecasting errors by 35% and cut inventory costs by 18%.

## How to Choose the Right AI Agent Builder for Your Team [...] • Built-In NLU: Intent classification, slot filling, and entity extraction out of the box

• Version Control and CLI: Git-like versioning and CLI deployment for multi-developer collaboration

• Plugin Marketplace: Community-built extensions for CRM integrations, analytics, and custom channels

Pros:

• Unmatched flexibility: you can customize literally every layer of the agent's behavior with code

• Open-core means no vendor lock-in, and you can inspect the underlying logic

• BYO-model support future-proofs against LLM pricing and capability shifts

Cons:

• Steep learning curve for non-developers; the UI assumes you're comfortable with APIs and code

• The free tier's $5 AI credit burns through quickly during testing with GPT-4o

results.title: 7 Best AI Agent Builders for Developers
results.content:
## Code-first vs. visual AI agent builders: Which is best for you?

As noted, most AI agent builders fall into one of two camps: code-first and visual. The one that’s best for your team depends on your needs and what each approach is best suited for.

For example, LangChain and CrewAI are code-first frameworks that offer full control over prompts, memory, and multiagent workflows, along with some visual hybrid options. Teams that need the most robust control but also want some low/no-code functionality should select one of these.

AutoGen is also code-first, but it adds multiagent orchestration with an optional but limited visual builder—it’s not ideal if your team needs visual support. [...] Ease of use: Overwhelming interface, but building AI agents is relatively straightforward. The agent's preview feature is especially useful for debugging, clearly showing tool calls, parameters, and outputs.
 Flexibility and robustness: Handles structured workflows, branching logic, and contextual responses well. However, the visual builder is limited to Google's Gemini models. For more control or external integrations, you'll need the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and external tools. Less flexible than FlowiseAI but has better security features.
 Performance: Highly responsive and scalable on Google Cloud. Model/API latency may vary, but overall performance is good. The playbook-based canvas stays clean and manageable, even for complex agents. [...] ### CrewAI approach and developer experience

CrewAI is built for multiagent coordination. "Agents" (LLM-powered AI workers) use "tools" (eg search or custom functions) to complete specific "tasks." These agents operate within a crew that manages their interactions and execution flow using a defined "process," either step by step (where processes orchestrate the execution of tasks by agents with a predefined strategy) or via a manager agent (an agent designated as manager\_agent in a crew to manage other agents). Individual prompts guide each agent's reasoning, enabling the team to work toward shared goals.

results.title: These 7 AI Agents Will Replace Your Entire Team (Perplexity Computer, Devin, Scribe, Claude CoWork)
results.content:
# These 7 AI Agents Will Replace Your Entire Team (Perplexity Computer, Devin, Scribe, Claude CoWork)
## Ben Angel
19400 subscribers
24 likes

### Description
1582 views
Posted: 13 Mar 2026
7 AI agents that execute work while you sleep: Perplexity Computer, Claude Cowork, Manus AI, Devin, NotebookLM, Atlas Agent Mode, and Scribe. Autonomy vs automation.

In this video, bestselling author Ben Angel (The Wolf Is at the Door) breaks down 7 powerful AI agents that can research, write, design, code, analyze data, and run business workflows while you sleep — without constant supervision.

These tools are transforming how one-person businesses, solopreneurs, and startups operate in 2026.

If you’re building a business with AI, these are the autonomous AI systems that will replace entire teams. [...] Here are the prompts that you're going to need. Screenshot these or grab them from my bio. This one to predict viral trends and generate leads. This one to find unrealized business hiding in your Instagram comments and DMs. And this one to research podcasts you can get featured on. And finally, number one, Scribe. This isn't as much an AI agent as it is a powerful tool to support your agents running. Every tool I just showed you is only as powerful as the system you build around it. If you can't document your workflow, you can't scale, period. And because these agents are so powerful, here's why you don't want to miss this one. Scribe watches your screen while you work and turns your actions into a step-by-step guide. Screenshots, clicks, directions. You explain it once, share the link, [...] those prompts can launch, systems that execute work and troubleshoot themselves. And that brings me to the first play in the AI black book. We're starting with the one that changes everything, Perplexity computer. This isn't a chatbot, it's a digital project manager with 19 different AI models working under it. You describe an outcome, like build me a competitive analysis of 50 companies, and it breaks that down into subtasks, assigns each one to the best AI model for the job, and then runs them in parallel in the cloud while you're at dinner. The best part is that you can schedule certain tasks to run at a set time each week. Perplexity used this to build a 4,000-row spreadsheet overnight that would have taken a human team a full week. It connects to over 400 services, remembers your

results.title: 8 Best AI Agent Tools Leading the 2025 AI Boom
results.content:
On the other hand, there are still some cases where developers want to go “tool-free” to save costs and facilitate customization. Without using an AI agent platform, companies can still build and manage autonomous AI agents by themselves using the combination of a programming language (e.g., Python or JavaScript), an LLM API (e.g., OpenAI GPT, Anthropic Claude, or Mistral), and a unique architecture.

However, it is not recommended as self-development is a very challenging process. Building your own AI agent without the help of any tool means that you and your AI teammates must: [...] The clearer your goal, the easier it is to pick the right tool. For example, even though there are many different tools out there, if you choose one of the options provided above, the answer would be:

### Consider Your Team’s Technical Skills

Having a particular passion for a particular AI agent framework is one thing, but whether your development team is using that tool appropriately is another story. No matter how good a tool is, if no one knows how to optimize it, it won’t bring any value to the project. That’s why you need to think about who will build and manage the custom AI agent.
